About

Born from the Land.
Built on Story.

Teetl'it Gwich'in. Whitehorse, Yukon.

Roots

Teetl'it Gwich'in, Fort McPherson

Bobbi Rose grew up connected to Teetl'it Gwich'in territory — the lands and waterways around Fort McPherson, NWT, known in the language as Teetl'it Zheh. Her family's relationship with the Peel River watershed, the Mackenzie Delta, and the boreal landscape runs deep, woven through generations of knowledge, language, and seasonal rhythms. That connection to place is the foundation of everything she does.

On the Land

The River as Teacher

Bobbi Rose came to guiding not as a profession but as a practice — a way of being in relationship with the land and water that shaped her. As a river guide with Dinjii Zhuh Adventures, she leads journeys on the rivers of Yukon and NWT that are fundamentally about more than logistics. They are about listening, about being a guest on living water, about letting place teach.

Her guiding brings together Indigenous ecological knowledge, land stewardship, storytelling, and genuine hospitality. She has guided for Nahanni River Adventures and trained emerging Indigenous guides through the Arctic Inspiration Prize–supported River Guide Training Program.

She is also a recognized voice in the academic and policy conversation around Indigenous land-based tourism, contributing to research on how guiding can serve both cultural continuity and ecological protection.

Dinjii Zhuh Adventures ↗ Nahanni Guide Profile ↗

Teaching & Mentorship

Passing the Knowledge Forward

One of Bobbi Rose's most consistent commitments is to the next generation. Through Indigenous Youth River Guide Training programs supported by the Arctic Inspiration Prize, she has helped young Gwich'in and northern youth build confidence, skills, and identity through time on the water.

Her mentorship work extends beyond guiding into entrepreneurship, cultural leadership, and creative practice. She has participated in EntrepreNorth programming and continues to be a resource for youth finding their footing between cultures, between cities and land, between tradition and innovation.

River Guide Training Program ↗ EntrepreNorth Workshops ↗

Arctic Inspiration Prize

Indigenous Youth River Guide Training

A prize-supported initiative bringing young Indigenous people onto the rivers of the north, building skills, identity, and cultural connection through land-based learning.

View Program PDF ↗

Creative Practice

Art, Fashion, and Cultural Expression

Creativity has always been part of Bobbi Rose's practice — from beadwork rooted in Gwich'in aesthetic tradition to fashion collaborations that explore Indigenous identity in contemporary contexts. Her River Beautyyy project and collaboration with Yukon Apparel bring northern identity into wearable form.

Her social presence — primarily through Instagram and TikTok as @river.beautyyy — is not just personal sharing but a deliberate act of visibility: showing what it looks like to be Gwich'in, to be from the north, to love the land and the water and also love fashion and creativity and your community.

This is not a contradiction. It is exactly the point.

Ready to connect?

Whether you're interested in guiding, speaking, collaboration, or mentorship — Bobbi Rose welcomes the conversation.

Get in Touch

Bobbi Rose

Teetl'it Gwich'in. Culture. Land. Rivers.

River guide, mentor, artist, and land protector based in Whitehorse, Yukon. Owner of Dinjii Zhuh Adventures. Carrying Gwich'in knowledge forward through every river journey and story shared.

hello@bobbirose.ca
